perturbation.original_text: Any disclosure of Confidential Information to any person or corporation for any commercial or other purpose not necessary for the conduct of the Society’s business shall be dee med Improper Disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Any disclosure of Confidential Information related to financial transactions to any person or corporation for any commercial or other purpose not necessary for the conduct of the Society’s business shall be deemed Improper Disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 2 focuses 'Improper Disclosure' only on financial transactions related Confidential Information, creating a contradiction as Section 3 references all Confidential Information when discussing obligations, creating uncertainty on if the disclosure must involve financial transactions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in st rictest confidence for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society. Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name. Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Improper Disclosure' broadly for any Confidential Information. The changed text narrows this definition to only Confidential Information 'related to financial transactions'. The contradicted text states that the 'Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence', without specifying the information has to be related to financial transactions, creating a contradiction because now it is ambiguous whether the confidentiality obligations in the contradicted text apply to all confidential information or only financial transactions, which directly impacts the enforceability and interpretation of the contract. This aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in st rictest confidence for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall endeavor to hold and maintain Confidential Information in reasonable confidence for the benefit of the Society.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall hold and maintain in strictest confidence' to 'shall endeavor to hold and maintain in reasonable confidence' in Section 3 weakens the obligation. This creates a contradiction with Section 5 regarding penalties, because Section 5 is directly implying strict confidence for penalties to take place, due to an Improper Disclosure
perturbation.contradicted_text: If proven Improper Disclosure sha ll take place, the Receiving Party’s membership in the Society shall be terminated immediately. Depending on circumstances, there may also be recourse to the courts and criminal or civil action, or both, may be initiated by the Society or by injured parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party 'shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence', whereas the changed text weakens this obligation to 'shall endeavor to hold and maintain Confidential Information in reasonable confidence'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'If proven Improper Disclosure shall take place, the Receiving Party’s membership in the Society shall be terminated immediately'. The original strictest confidence clause aligns with the penalty for Improper Disclosure, implying a high standard of care. The changed, weakened obligation makes the threshold for 'Improper Disclosure' ambiguous and the immediate termination penalty less justifiable, creating a strong contradiction. The definition for Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction states that it occurs when key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear, which directly applies here.

perturbation.original_text: The nondisclosure provision of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party’s duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.changed_text: The nondisclosure provision of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement; however, Receiving Party’s duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall only remain in effect for two years following termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 4 limits the duration of the confidentiality obligation to two years post-termination, directly contradicting the original 'indefinitely'. This creates a contradiction with Section 3, which states about deleting 'Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in st rictest confidence for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society. Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name. Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the nondisclosure provision survives indefinitely, while the changed text limits it to two years. The contradicted text states that confidential information stored electronically will be deleted upon termination of membership. While the deletion of electronically stored information upon termination might seem to align with a two-year limit, the initial requirement to hold and maintain confidential information in the strictest confidence for the 'sole and exclusive purposes of the Society' and not for the benefit of the receiving party for an indefinite period creates a direct conflict when the duration is shortened to two years. The receiving party's obligation to protect the information is now limited, creating ambiguity and a direct contradiction with the initial indefinite obligation.

perturbation.original_text: If proven Improper Disclosure sha ll take place, the Receiving Party’s membership in the Society shall be terminated immediately.
perturbation.changed_text: If proven Improper Disclosure shall take place, the Receiving Party’s membership in the Society may be terminated at the Society’s discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be terminated immediately' to 'may be terminated at the Society's discretion' in Section 5 introduces ambiguity and weakens the penalty. This creates a direct contradiction with the firm obligation outlined previously in Section 3 to keep information confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in st rictest confidence for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society. Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name. Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that improper disclosure 'shall be terminated immediately', creating a firm and mandatory obligation. The changed text replaces this with 'may be terminated at the Society's discretion', introducing optionality and discretion where there was none before. This directly contradicts the 'Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence' obligation in the contradicted text, as the penalty for violating that strict confidence is now discretionary, weakening the obligation and creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Depending on circumstances, there may also be recourse to the courts and criminal or civil action, or both, may be initiated by the Society or by injured parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Under no circumstances, there will be a recourse to the courts and criminal or civil action, or both, may be initiated by the Society or by injured parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 5 now prohibits any recourse to the courts for Improper Disclosure. This creates an contradiction with Section 3 that mentions 'Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name', now, Section 5 forbids any legal recourses to these harms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in st rictest confidence for the sole and exclusive purposes of the Society. Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name. Upon termination of membership, Receiving Party will immediately return to the Society all records, notes and other written, printed or tangible materi al in its possession, including CDs and DVDs pertaining to Confidential In formation. Confidential Information stored electronically in computers or any other electronic devices will be deleted upon membership termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 states "Under no circumstances, there will be a recourse to the courts," which directly contradicts the Receiving Party's obligation detailed in the contradicted text. Specifically, the contradicted text states that the "Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information for their own benefit or permit the use of Confidential Information by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Society or its good name." The changed text prevents the Society from taking legal action against the Receiving Party if they do act to the detriment of the Society, thus creating a direct conflict and making it unenforceable. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists as it is a clear and direct conflict.
